You are reviewing merge 446a212 (docs(m11): fix risks.md §A.2 rig.rs scope conflict) on master in nostr-multi-platform. This fixes a scope conflict in risks.md §A.2 regarding rig.rs. Doctrine D0-D5:
- D0: kernel never grows app nouns
- D1: best-effort rendering
- D2: negentropy first
- D3: outbox routing automatic
- D4: single writer per fact
- D5: snapshots bounded

File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 LOC hard.

diff --git a/docs/design/podcast/risks.md b/docs/design/podcast/risks.md
index 888cc2d..a4a827f 100644
--- a/docs/design/podcast/risks.md
+++ b/docs/design/podcast/risks.md
@@ -22,7 +22,7 @@
 - **Severity**: Medium-High.
 - **Why**: The reference app's entire LLM surface uses `FoundationModels.LanguageModelSession`. On iOS we route through `AppleIntelligenceCapability` (free, on-device). On Android/Desktop/Web (post-M11) we must route through `rig.rs`, which means: a provider, an API key, network calls, cost. The UX divergence is real — Ask becomes a fundamentally different feature off-iOS.
 - **Mitigation**:
-  1. M11 ships iOS only — divergence is theoretical until M15.
+  1. M11 ships iOS only **and** requires the rig.rs path to land and be CI-tested against a real LLM endpoint (see `podcast-llm.md` §K). Divergence across platforms is real post-M11, but the rig.rs path is demonstrated in M11, not deferred to M15.
   2. The router lives in Rust (`podcast-llm/src/router.rs`) so the policy is centrally testable.
   3. Document divergence + behavioral expectations in `docs/perf/m15/cross-platform.md` (deferred).
   4. Settings surface (post-M11) lets the user pick "Local (Apple)" / "OpenAI" / "Anthropic" / "Local (Ollama URL)" — same code path, different routing.

Verify the fix is accurate and does not introduce new inconsistencies, no TODO/FIXME, no size violations. Be terse.

     141 docs/design/podcast/risks.md

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# Risks + open questions
     2	
     3	> Parent: [`../podcast-app-rebuild.md`](../podcast-app-rebuild.md).
     4	> Combined per AGENTS.md — keep sub-docs ≤ 500 LOC.
     5	
     6	---
     7	
     8	## A. Risks (high → low)
     9	
    10	### A.1 sqlite-vec on iOS — static linking with `load_extension`
    11	
    12	- **Severity**: High. Top risk in the whole M11 stack.
    13	- **Why**: `rusqlite` with `bundled` compiles `libsqlite3` from source, which is fine. Loading `vec0` at runtime requires `SQLITE_ENABLE_LOAD_EXTENSION`. iOS App Store review historically rejects `dlopen` of arbitrary dylibs; the safer path is **static link** of the vec0 symbols into the libsqlite3 we build, behind `SQLITE_CORE`.
    14	- **Mitigation**:
    15	  1. Spike before Step 1C: build a minimal Rust+rusqlite+sqlite-vec crate, archive for `aarch64-apple-ios`, embed in a throwaway xcframework, install on iPhone 12, run a 1000-vector insert + search round-trip.
    16	  2. Document the `build.rs` patch (cflags + statically registered extension) in `docs/design/podcast/podcast-rag.md`.
    17	  3. **Fallback**: if static link is infeasible, ship a Rust-side `instant-distance` HNSW backend behind the same `VectorStore` trait. Schema persists to a flat `.bin` file instead of SQLite. Worse cold-start but unblocks M11.
    18	- **Owner**: assigned at M11 kickoff; spike result is a blocker for `podcast-rag` impl beginning.
    19	
    20	### A.2 Apple Intelligence is iOS-only → cross-platform divergence
    21	
    22	- **Severity**: Medium-High.
    23	- **Why**: The reference app's entire LLM surface uses `FoundationModels.LanguageModelSession`. On iOS we route through `AppleIntelligenceCapability` (free, on-device). On Android/Desktop/Web (post-M11) we must route through `rig.rs`, which means: a provider, an API key, network calls, cost. The UX divergence is real — Ask becomes a fundamentally different feature off-iOS.
    24	- **Mitigation**:
    25	  1. M11 ships iOS only **and** requires the rig.rs path to land and be CI-tested against a real LLM endpoint (see `podcast-llm.md` §K). Divergence across platforms is real post-M11, but the rig.rs path is demonstrated in M11, not deferred to M15.
    26	  2. The router lives in Rust (`podcast-llm/src/router.rs`) so the policy is centrally testable.
    27	  3. Document divergence + behavioral expectations in `docs/perf/m15/cross-platform.md` (deferred).
    28	  4. Settings surface (post-M11) lets the user pick "Local (Apple)" / "OpenAI" / "Anthropic" / "Local (Ollama URL)" — same code path, different routing.
    29	
    30	### A.3 Foundation Models prompt-shape parity vs `rig.rs`
    31	
    32	- **Severity**: Medium.
    33	- **Why**: The Foundation Models API takes one big string + optional system prompt; `rig.rs` likes a structured `Vec<ChatMessage>` with role tags. The same prompt routed two ways can produce divergent output if the framing changes ("User: ... Assistant: ..." in-string vs proper message roles).
    34	- **Mitigation**:
    35	  1. `podcast-llm/src/prompts.rs` defines each prompt as both a flat string (for AppleIntel) AND a `Vec<rig::completion::Message>` (for rig). Both shapes are byte-deterministic functions of the same inputs.
    36	  2. Golden tests: same input → both shapes → snapshotted; reviewed when changed.
    37	  3. Where output format matters (the `CHAPTER|...|YES` parsing in `extractChapters`, the `FOUND|MM:SS|...` in `findRelevantTimestamp`), the parser is a single Rust function that handles both routes' outputs. Tests cover each route's raw output samples.
    38	
    39	### A.4 Embedding-dimension swap mid-project
    40	
    41	- **Severity**: Medium.
    42	- **Why**: We choose `bge-small-en-v1.5` at 384 dims. Future requests for `bge-large-en-v1.5` (1024 dims) or `text-embedding-3-large` (3072 dims) require a sqlite-vec schema migration that rebuilds every existing index. Each embedding takes ~12 ms on iPhone 12; 5,000 chunks per podcast × 100 podcasts × 12 ms = 100 minutes of background re-index per device.
    43	- **Mitigation**:
    44	  1. Lock 384 dims in M11. Schema records the model name.
    45	  2. Migration is an explicit `RebuildEmbeddingIndex` action with progress UI.
    46	  3. Tests for the migration include the worst-case (500k vectors) and assert wall-clock budget.
    47	
    48	### A.5 Background audio reliability on iPhone 12
    49	
    50	- **Severity**: Medium.
    51	- **Why**: `AVAudioSession` interactions with the kernel's actor crossing FFI on every `Tick` event can introduce jitter. The reference Swift app handles this in-process; we cross FFI ~4×/s plus on every state change. On a battery-throttled iPhone 12, this is non-zero.
    52	- **Mitigation**:
    53	  1. The bridge throttles `Tick` to ≤ 4 Hz at the source (per [`capabilities.md`](capabilities.md) §A).
    54	  2. The Rust-side coalescer is the second line of defense (per ADR-0002).
    55	  3. Hardware perf test in [`exit-gate.md`](exit-gate.md) §C catches regressions.
    56	
    57	### A.6 `feed-rs` Podcasting 2.0 gaps
    58	
    59	- **Severity**: Low-Medium.
    60	- **Why**: `feed-rs` doesn't expose `<podcast:transcript>` etc. We layer a bespoke walker over the raw XML. Edge-case malformed feeds may break our walker.
    61	- **Mitigation**:
    62	  1. Golden fixtures for 5+ real Podcasting 2.0 feeds in `tests/fixtures/`.
    63	  2. Walker errors are non-fatal: missing P2.0 extensions don't fail `FetchFeed`; the episode is parsed with the basic fields only.
    64	  3. Future: replace with a maintained P2.0 Rust crate if one emerges.
    65	
    66	### A.7 Codegen drift between branches
    67	
    68	- **Severity**: Low-Medium.
    69	- **Why**: 7 parallel lanes regenerate `nmp-app-podcast/` on every module change. Merge conflicts in the generated `action.rs`/`update.rs`/`view_spec.rs` are predictable and painful.
    70	- **Mitigation**:
    71	  1. Generated files are committed (per ADR-0010) — conflicts surface at merge.
    72	  2. CI runs `nmp gen modules --check` post-merge; rebases that produce stale generated code fail the build.
    73	  3. Convention: generated files are merge-with-"theirs" then `nmp gen modules` re-run, never hand-edited.
    74	
    75	### A.8 Reference Swift app commits drifting
    76	
    77	- **Severity**: Low.
    78	- **Why**: `/Users/pablofernandez/src/podcast` may be edited during M11. Our screenshot baselines + view copies depend on a specific SHA.
    79	- **Mitigation**: per [`copy.md`](copy.md) §3, we pin the source SHA in `docs/perf/m11/parity-screenshots.md`. Re-baselining is deliberate.
    80	
    81	### A.9 Parallel-agent codegen conflicts
    82	
    83	- **Severity**: Low.
    84	- **Why**: Two agents wiring two lanes both regenerate bindings. Same as A.7 but at the Swift binding layer (`bindings/swift/` checked-in). Possible "phantom" Swift diffs from generator nondeterminism.
    85	- **Mitigation**: deterministic codegen is a hard ADR-0010 requirement. CI determinism test catches regressions immediately.
    86	
    87	### A.10 Insight voice-recording → transcription pipeline reliability
    88	
    89	- **Severity**: Low.
    90	- **Why**: The Insight capture flow is 5 steps (record → transcribe thought → match excerpt → embed → write). Any step failing produces an orphan recording. Reference app has the same issue.
    91	- **Mitigation**: action ledger atomicity. Each step is a ledger step; on failure, the cleanup is `DeleteOrphanRecording { recording_id }`. Restart recovery resumes from the failed step.
    92	
    93	---
    94	
    95	## B. Open questions (for ADRs)
    96	
    97	### B.1 Should `ChatSessionState` be a DomainModule or a projection cache?
    98	
    99	- **Context**: Chat history can be many turns × many tokens. It's expensive to persist on every token but useful to persist across kill-relaunch.
   100	- **Options**: (a) DomainModule with batched writes (every 50 tokens or 5 s); (b) Projection cache, evaporates on kill — re-fetchable only if the LLM provider keeps a server-side history.
   101	- **Recommendation**: (a) with snapshot writes. Future ADR.
   102	
   103	### B.2 Where does the Podcasting 2.0 V4V (`<podcast:value>`) data live?
   104	
   105	- **Context**: V4V/Lightning splits could feed into a future zaps integration (M12). Today we have nowhere to put them.
   106	- **Options**: (a) Persist as `EpisodeRecord.value_block: Option<ValueBlock>`; (b) Dropped at parse time; (c) Separate `Podcast2ValuesModule` domain.
   107	- **Recommendation**: (a). Forward-compatible; minimal cost. Future ADR if it grows.
   108	
   109	### B.3 Should transcript-tap-to-play live in `TranscriptViewModule` or as a sibling `TranscriptInteractionModule`?
   110	
   111	- **Context**: Tapping a chunk dispatches `Seek`. That's a one-line dispatcher — overkill for a separate module.
   112	- **Recommendation**: in-place in `TranscriptViewModule`. No ADR needed.
   113	
   114	### B.4 Podcast Index keys — store in `KeyValueStoreCapability` or hardcode for the demo?
   115	
   116	- **Context**: M11 needs Discover to work for the demo. The reference app reads from Info.plist or env. We don't want to ship demo keys.
   117	- **Options**: (a) Settings flow to enter keys (requires Settings view enhancement beyond parity); (b) `.env` file in dev; (c) Build-time bake from `nmp.toml` `[secrets]` section, gitignored.
   118	- **Recommendation**: (b) for M11 dev, (a) post-M11 in a Settings expansion. Future ADR.
   119	
   120	### B.5 Insight thought audio playback — Bridge or capability?
   121	
   122	- **Context**: A short transient `AVPlayer` plays a recorded thought when the user taps "Play recording" on an InsightCard. Currently planned as Bridge-only (UI plays it, kernel doesn't know).
   123	- **Options**: (a) Bridge-only; (b) extend `AudioPlaybackCapability` to support a secondary mini-player; (c) new `MicroAudioCapability`.
   124	- **Recommendation**: (a). It's pure UI behavior and the Rust side doesn't care about which moment it plays. ADR only if (b) or (c) becomes necessary.
   125	
   126	### B.6 Episode duration source of truth
   127	
   128	- **Context**: RSS feeds publish `<itunes:duration>`. AVPlayer reports `currentItem.duration`. They can differ (rare, but documented).
   129	- **Options**: (a) Always trust the feed; (b) Always trust AVPlayer; (c) Prefer feed, update from AVPlayer if difference > 5%.
   130	- **Recommendation**: (c). ADR cheap; the impl is small.
   131	
   132	### B.7 OPML import — additive scope decision documented; what about export?
   133	
   134	- **Context**: M11 adds `ImportOpml` as additive. Export is a similar one-action win.
   135	- **Recommendation**: `ExportOpml` follows in M11 if Settings UI allows (the existing Settings has no Import/Export section; adding one is one-screen). Defer to a Settings ADR. M11 ships Import only.
   136	
   137	### B.8 Multi-language transcription default
   138	
   139	- **Context**: `TranscriptionService.swift` defaults to `en-US`. Auto-detect is non-trivial and SpeechAnalyzer doesn't help.
   140	- **Options**: (a) Always en-US; (b) Settings "Default Language"; (c) Per-podcast override.
   141	- **Recommendation**: (a) for M11; (b) added in a Settings expansion. ADR if (c) becomes important.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1552:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1257: | 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1405:+- `selectOptimalRelays` (`helpers/relay-selection.ts:14-93`) is a greedy set-cover: each iteration picks the relay covering the most uncovered users, optionally biased by `score(relay, coverage, popularity)`. `maxRelaysPerUser` caps per-user concentration. The TODO at line 89 acknowledges that the final per-user relay list may exceed `maxRelaysPerUser` because the count is enforced during selection, not after.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1800:+The TODO at line 89 acknowledges a known imprecision: `maxRelaysPerUser` is enforced during the greedy pass but not as a post-filter, so a user can end up with more than `maxRelaysPerUser` final relays if their first picks happened to cover the pool.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2416:+Source explicitly notes the gap: `// TODO: The state of this tracker needs to be added to cache adapters` (`tracker.ts:48-49`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2446:+`calculateRelaySetFromEvent` doesn't fully account for relays where specific hashtags are popular (`relay/sets/calculate.ts:18-19` TODO). For tag-heavy posts (e.g., #bitdevs), publishing is suboptimal.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2450:+`getTopRelaysForAuthors` has TODO to incorporate quality metrics. Currently ranks by author-count only.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2696:+Outbox tracker state is *not* persisted to cache adapters — there is a `TODO` in `tracker.ts:48-49` noting rehydration is unimplemented. On every fresh app boot, the tracker is empty and the first wave of subscriptions pays full discovery cost.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3096:5. TODO/FIXME/unimplemented sneaking into prose or code?

docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3256:docs/design/podcast/copy.md:281:- **`// MARK: NMP-WIRE — TODO` blocks are the only allowed change** to a view file before it is wired. Any other source change requires an explicit ADR + a parity-screenshots.md note with reviewer sign-off.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:603:+**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:621:+- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Each pre-existing one is either resolved or has an ADR justifying the deferral with a tracking issue.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:1162:40:+**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1778:docs/plan.md:363:- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1648:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3161:-- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1649:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3162:+- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1650:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3176:Review for: (1) doctrine compliance, (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented sneaking in, (3) test coverage where tests should exist, (4) file-size compliance (any new file > 300 LOC?), (5) docs internal consistency (M10.5 gate, M11 pod-NIP language, etc), (6) hidden shortcuts. Be terse. If fine, say so. If concern, name file:line + fix.

M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2099:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:1161:58:+- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Each pre-existing one is either resolved or has an ADR justifying the deferral with a tracking issue.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:218:./docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:221:./docs/research/ndk/missing-features-for-nmp.md:36:Source explicitly notes the gap: `// TODO: The state of this tracker needs to be added to cache adapters` (`tracker.ts:48-49`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:222:./docs/research/ndk/missing-features-for-nmp.md:66:`calculateRelaySetFromEvent` doesn't fully account for relays where specific hashtags are popular (`relay/sets/calculate.ts:18-19` TODO). For tag-heavy posts (e.g., #bitdevs), publishing is suboptimal.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:223:./docs/research/ndk/missing-features-for-nmp.md:70:`getTopRelaysForAuthors` has TODO to incorporate quality metrics. Currently ranks by author-count only.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:224:./docs/research/ndk/outbox.md:98:Outbox tracker state is *not* persisted to cache adapters — there is a `TODO` in `tracker.ts:48-49` noting rehydration is unimplemented. On every fresh app boot, the tracker is empty and the first wave of subscriptions pays full discovery cost.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:225:./docs/research/applesauce/event-store-query-builders.md:138:- `selectOptimalRelays` (`helpers/relay-selection.ts:14-93`) is a greedy set-cover: each iteration picks the relay covering the most uncovered users, optionally biased by `score(relay, coverage, popularity)`. `maxRelaysPerUser` caps per-user concentration. The TODO at line 89 acknowledges that the final per-user relay list may exceed `maxRelaysPerUser` because the count is enforced during selection, not after.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:226:./docs/research/applesauce/outbox.md:84:The TODO at line 89 acknowledges a known imprecision: `maxRelaysPerUser` is enforced during the greedy pass but not as a post-filter, so a user can end up with more than `maxRelaysPerUser` final relays if their first picks happened to cover the pool.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:227:./docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:88:- **Two fixture apps prove the boundary in v1.** A tiny non-Nostr-shaped fixture (e.g., a TODO/notes module with an app-local identity) lands in 1a.1; the Twitter clone is the first Nostr-shaped extension module. Together they prove the kernel works in both directions.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:276:./docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:7:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:277:./docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:25:- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:319:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:189:+- `selectOptimalRelays` (`helpers/relay-selection.ts:14-93`) is a greedy set-cover: each iteration picks the relay covering the most uncovered users, optionally biased by `score(relay, coverage, popularity)`. `maxRelaysPerUser` caps per-user concentration. The TODO at line 89 acknowledges that the final per-user relay list may exceed `maxRelaysPerUser` because the count is enforced during selection, not after.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:320:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:584:+The TODO at line 89 acknowledges a known imprecision: `maxRelaysPerUser` is enforced during the greedy pass but not as a post-filter, so a user can end up with more than `maxRelaysPerUser` final relays if their first picks happened to cover the pool.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:321:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1200:+Source explicitly notes the gap: `// TODO: The state of this tracker needs to be added to cache adapters` (`tracker.ts:48-49`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:322:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1230:+`calculateRelaySetFromEvent` doesn't fully account for relays where specific hashtags are popular (`relay/sets/calculate.ts:18-19` TODO). For tag-heavy posts (e.g., #bitdevs), publishing is suboptimal.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:323:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1234:+`getTopRelaysForAuthors` has TODO to incorporate quality metrics. Currently ranks by author-count only.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:324:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1480:+Outbox tracker state is *not* persisted to cache adapters — there is a `TODO` in `tracker.ts:48-49` noting rehydration is unimplemented. On every fresh app boot, the tracker is empty and the first wave of subscriptions pays full discovery cost.

All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:378:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2386:++**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:379:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2404:++- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Each pre-existing one is either resolved or has an ADR justifying the deferral with a tracking issue.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:384:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2631:+docs/plan.md:21:- **Fixture proving the kernel boundary** in `crates/fixture-todo-core` (~304 LOC) plus generated `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture`: a non-Nostr TODO module implementing all five substrate trait families, with codegen producing the per-app crate. Proves the kernel works for arbitrary domains, not just Nostr.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:385:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2633:+docs/plan.md:77:**Demo product:** `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture` — a TODO list app driven by the kernel substrate with no Nostr concepts in it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:386:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2635:+docs/plan.md:345:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:387:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2637:+docs/plan.md:363:- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Each pre-existing one is either resolved or has an ADR justifying the deferral with a tracking issue.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:403:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3135:+The FFI and iOS bridge code is **clean of blocking debt markers** (TODO/FIXME/unimplemented/panic). docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:404:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3161:-- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Each pre-existing one is either resolved or has an ADR justifying the deferral with a tracking issue.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:405:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3162:+- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:406:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3176:Review for: (1) doctrine compliance, (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented sneaking in, (3) test coverage where tests should exist, (4) file-size compliance (any new file > 300 LOC?), (5) docs internal consistency (M10.5 gate, M11 pod-NIP language, etc), (6) hidden shortcuts. Be terse. If fine, say so. If concern, name file:line + fix.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:464:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:240:-- **1a.1** **Kernel substrate prototype + non-Nostr fixture** — the five extension trait families (`DomainModule`, `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `CapabilityModule`, `IdentityModule`); composite reverse index; delta buffer with coalescing; claim-based GC; codegen producing a working `nmp-app-fixture` crate from a `fixture-todo-core` app module that implements all five trait families; desktop iced shell rendering a TODO list. ~2 weeks. **Proves: kernel substrate works for non-Nostr-shaped data; codegen pipeline functional.**

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:475:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:73:+**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:499:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1822:+docs/plan.md:363:- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:500:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1984:+docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:88:- **Two fixture apps prove the boundary in v1.** A tiny non-Nostr-shaped fixture (e.g., a TODO/notes module with an app-local identity) lands in 1a.1; the Twitter clone is the first Nostr-shaped extension module. Together they prove the kernel works in both directions.

Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:509:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5903:+docs/plan.md:21:- **Fixture proving the kernel boundary** in `crates/fixture-todo-core` (~304 LOC) plus generated `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture`: a non-Nostr TODO module implementing all five substrate trait families, with codegen producing the per-app crate. docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:510:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5907:+docs/plan.md:77:**Demo product:** `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture` — a TODO list app driven by the kernel substrate with no Nostr concepts in it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:511:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5914:+docs/plan.md:345:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:512:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5916:+docs/plan.md:363:- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:513:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5925:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1567:Review for: (1) doctrine compliance (in particular D0 — does nmp-nip65 propose any kernel-side type leaks? D4 — single source for relay-set facts?); (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented; (3) file-size compliance (any new file > 300 LOC?); (4) internal consistency across the 8 sub-docs; (5) compatibility with the upcoming M3 LMDB schema (e.g. watermark table); (6) the planned test (m2_subscription_compilation_audit) exit criteria; (7) any hidden shortcuts. Be terse; name file:line + fix for any concern.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:559:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6149:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3423:docs/plan.md:21:- **Fixture proving the kernel boundary** in `crates/fixture-todo-core` (~304 LOC) plus generated `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture`: a non-Nostr TODO module implementing all five substrate trait families, with codegen producing the per-app crate. Proves the kernel works for arbitrary domains, not just Nostr.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:560:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6150:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3424:docs/plan.md:77:**Demo product:** `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture` — a TODO list app driven by the kernel substrate with no Nostr concepts in it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:561:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6151:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3426:docs/plan.md:345:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:562:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6152:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3427:docs/plan.md:363:- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:601:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6425:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1388:Review: (1) doctrine compliance (in particular D0 + D2 — composite reverse index + working-set bound must remain enforced through the trait); (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented; (3) file-size compliance; (4) consistency of EventStore trait surface across the 6 sub-docs; (5) the choice of nostr-lmdb as on-disk byte store vs roll-our-own — is the gap analysis honest?; (6) the GC eviction algorithm + budget; (7) the migration plumbing's testability; (8) any hidden shortcuts. Be terse; name file:line + fix for any concern.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:602:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6441:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:675:+- The placeholder block is fenced by `// MARK: NMP-WIRE` (start) and either `// MARK: NMP-WIRE — wired` (after wiring) or `// MARK: NMP-WIRE — TODO` (still pending).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:610:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6462:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3055:docs/plan.md:345:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:666:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:8173:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:669:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1405:+- `selectOptimalRelays` (`helpers/relay-selection.ts:14-93`) is a greedy set-cover: each iteration picks the relay covering the most uncovered users, optionally biased by `score(relay, coverage, popularity)`. `maxRelaysPerUser` caps per-user concentration. The TODO at line 89 acknowledges that the final per-user relay list may exceed `maxRelaysPerUser` because the count is enforced during selection, not after.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:670:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1800:+The TODO at line 89 acknowledges a known imprecision: `maxRelaysPerUser` is enforced during the greedy pass but not as a post-filter, so a user can end up with more than `maxRelaysPerUser` final relays if their first picks happened to cover the pool.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:671:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2416:+Source explicitly notes the gap: `// TODO: The state of this tracker needs to be added to cache adapters` (`tracker.ts:48-49`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:672:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2446:+`calculateRelaySetFromEvent` doesn't fully account for relays where specific hashtags are popular (`relay/sets/calculate.ts:18-19` TODO). For tag-heavy posts (e.g., #bitdevs), publishing is suboptimal.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:673:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2450:+`getTopRelaysForAuthors` has TODO to incorporate quality metrics. Currently ranks by author-count only.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:674:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2696:+Outbox tracker state is *not* persisted to cache adapters — there is a `TODO` in `tracker.ts:48-49` noting rehydration is unimplemented. On every fresh app boot, the tracker is empty and the first wave of subscriptions pays full discovery cost.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:728:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1429:docs/plan.md:21:- **Fixture proving the kernel boundary** in `crates/fixture-todo-core` (~304 LOC) plus generated `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture`: a non-Nostr TODO module implementing all five substrate trait families, with codegen producing the per-app crate. Proves the kernel works for arbitrary domains, not just Nostr.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:729:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1433:docs/plan.md:77:**Demo product:** `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture` — a TODO list app driven by the kernel substrate with no Nostr concepts in it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:730:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1440:docs/plan.md:345:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:731:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1442:docs/plan.md:363:- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:732:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1451:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1567:Review for: (1) doctrine compliance (in particular D0 — does nmp-nip65 propose any kernel-side type leaks? D4 — single source for relay-set facts?); (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented; (3) file-size compliance (any new file > 300 LOC?); (4) internal consistency across the 8 sub-docs; (5) compatibility with the upcoming M3 LMDB schema (e.g. watermark table); (6) the planned test (m2_subscription_compilation_audit) exit criteria; (7) any hidden shortcuts. Be terse; name file:line + fix for any concern.

Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:735:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1476:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1940:docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:88:- **Two fixture apps prove the boundary in v1.** A tiny non-Nostr-shaped fixture (e.g., a TODO/notes module with an app-local identity) lands in 1a.1; the Twitter clone is the first Nostr-shaped extension module. Together they prove the kernel works in both directions.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:736:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1501:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4401:Other checks: no literal `TODO`, `FIXME`, or `unimplemented!` in the added docs. docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:745:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1607:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2386:++**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

Each pre-existing one is either resolved or has an ADR justifying the deferral with a tracking issue.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:853:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2071:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:601:+**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:891:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:1838:docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:25:- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.

Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/59f8d1b.md:158:docs/design/podcast/copy.md:167:// MARK: NMP-WIRE — TODO
docs/perf/codex-reviews/59f8d1b.md:159:docs/design/podcast/copy.md:281:- **`// MARK: NMP-WIRE — TODO` blocks are the only allowed change** to a view file before it is wired. Any other source change requires an explicit ADR + a parity-screenshots.md note with reviewer sign-off.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/59f8d1b.md:160:docs/design/podcast/copy.md:288:- `grep -RnE '// MARK: NMP-WIRE — TODO' ios/NmpPodcast/Views/ | wc -l` is the work-remaining counter for Step 7.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5914:+docs/plan.md:345:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6081:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2386:++**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6122:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3161:-- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6123:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3162:+- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6124:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3176:Review for: (1) doctrine compliance, (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented sneaking in, (3) test coverage where tests should exist, (4) file-size compliance (any new file > 300 LOC?), (5) docs internal consistency (M10.5 gate, M11 pod-NIP language, etc), (6) hidden shortcuts. Be terse. If fine, say so. If concern, name file:line + fix.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6572:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:1160:40:+**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:8173:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b4b6afa.md:13:No extra TODO/FIXME/unimplemented found. No file crossed 500 LOC hard limit. capabilities.md and podcast-core.md remained over 300 soft limit but this predated the merge. No new kernel/FFI ADR required.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/446a212.md:47:Verify the fix is accurate and does not introduce new inconsistencies, no TODO/FIXME, no size violations. Be terse.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:73:+**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

Each pre-existing one is either resolved or has an ADR justifying the deferral with a tracking issue.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:263:Review for: (1) doctrine compliance, (2) TODO/FIXME/unimplemented sneaking in, (3) file-size budget, (4) internal consistency with the plan's own stated scope. Be terse. If everything is fine, say so. If there is a real concern, name the file:line and what to fix.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:265:I’ll verify the merged file directly so the review can cite current line numbers rather than relying on the pasted diff. I’m checking the plan text, file length, and TODO-style markers against the stated doctrine/scope.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3161:-- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Each pre-existing one is either resolved or has an ADR justifying the deferral with a tracking issue.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3162:+- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3176:Review for: (1) doctrine compliance, (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented sneaking in, (3) test coverage where tests should exist, (4) file-size compliance (any new file > 300 LOC?), (5) docs internal consistency (M10.5 gate, M11 pod-NIP language, etc), (6) hidden shortcuts. Be terse. If fine, say so. If concern, name file:line + fix.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3423:docs/plan.md:21:- **Fixture proving the kernel boundary** in `crates/fixture-todo-core` (~304 LOC) plus generated `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture`: a non-Nostr TODO module implementing all five substrate trait families, with codegen producing the per-app crate. Proves the kernel works for arbitrary domains, not just Nostr.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3424:docs/plan.md:77:**Demo product:** `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture` — a TODO list app driven by the kernel substrate with no Nostr concepts in it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3426:docs/plan.md:345:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3427:docs/plan.md:363:- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:189:+- `selectOptimalRelays` (`helpers/relay-selection.ts:14-93`) is a greedy set-cover: each iteration picks the relay covering the most uncovered users, optionally biased by `score(relay, coverage, popularity)`. `maxRelaysPerUser` caps per-user concentration. The TODO at line 89 acknowledges that the final per-user relay list may exceed `maxRelaysPerUser` because the count is enforced during selection, not after.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:584:+The TODO at line 89 acknowledges a known imprecision: `maxRelaysPerUser` is enforced during the greedy pass but not as a post-filter, so a user can end up with more than `maxRelaysPerUser` final relays if their first picks happened to cover the pool.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1200:+Source explicitly notes the gap: `// TODO: The state of this tracker needs to be added to cache adapters` (`tracker.ts:48-49`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1230:+`calculateRelaySetFromEvent` doesn't fully account for relays where specific hashtags are popular (`relay/sets/calculate.ts:18-19` TODO). For tag-heavy posts (e.g., #bitdevs), publishing is suboptimal.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1234:+`getTopRelaysForAuthors` has TODO to incorporate quality metrics. Currently ranks by author-count only.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1480:+Outbox tracker state is *not* persisted to cache adapters — there is a `TODO` in `tracker.ts:48-49` noting rehydration is unimplemented. On every fresh app boot, the tracker is empty and the first wave of subscriptions pays full discovery cost.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1875:Review: (1) are the research findings internally consistent with NMP doctrine, (2) any TODO/FIXME, (3) file sizes, (4) any finding that contradicts the current plan or design. Be terse.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1877:I’ll verify the merge against the current repo docs, then check the added files for TODO/FIXME and line-count ceilings. I’m also doing a light memory pass for prior NMP/TENEX-style review context before relying on the workspace.

The TODO at line 89 acknowledges that the final per-user relay list may exceed `maxRelaysPerUser` because the count is enforced during selection, not after.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2126:docs/research/applesauce/outbox.md:84:The TODO at line 89 acknowledges a known imprecision: `maxRelaysPerUser` is enforced during the greedy pass but not as a post-filter, so a user can end up with more than `maxRelaysPerUser` final relays if their first picks happened to cover the pool.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2136:docs/research/ndk/outbox.md:98:Outbox tracker state is *not* persisted to cache adapters — there is a `TODO` in `tracker.ts:48-49` noting rehydration is unimplemented. On every fresh app boot, the tracker is empty and the first wave of subscriptions pays full discovery cost.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2196:docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:88:- **Two fixture apps prove the boundary in v1.** A tiny non-Nostr-shaped fixture (e.g., a TODO/notes module with an app-local identity) lands in 1a.1; the Twitter clone is the first Nostr-shaped extension module. Together they prove the kernel works in both directions.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2230:docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:483:1. Phase 1a.1 (kernel substrate prototype) ships with one fixture module (`fixture-todo-core`) demonstrating each of the five trait families. Codegen produces a working `nmp-app-fixture` crate. Desktop iced app renders a TODO list, no business logic in Swift / iced.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1277:+ - `grep -RnE '// MARK: NMP-WIRE — TODO' ios/NmpPodcast/Views/ | wc -l` is the work-remaining counter for Step 7.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1325:+ | 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1473:++- `selectOptimalRelays` (`helpers/relay-selection.ts:14-93`) is a greedy set-cover: each iteration picks the relay covering the most uncovered users, optionally biased by `score(relay, coverage, popularity)`. `maxRelaysPerUser` caps per-user concentration. The TODO at line 89 acknowledges that the final per-user relay list may exceed `maxRelaysPerUser` because the count is enforced during selection, not after.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1868:++The TODO at line 89 acknowledges a known imprecision: `maxRelaysPerUser` is enforced during the greedy pass but not as a post-filter, so a user can end up with more than `maxRelaysPerUser` final relays if their first picks happened to cover the pool.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2484:++Source explicitly notes the gap: `// TODO: The state of this tracker needs to be added to cache adapters` (`tracker.ts:48-49`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2514:++`calculateRelaySetFromEvent` doesn't fully account for relays where specific hashtags are popular (`relay/sets/calculate.ts:18-19` TODO). For tag-heavy posts (e.g., #bitdevs), publishing is suboptimal.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2518:++`getTopRelaysForAuthors` has TODO to incorporate quality metrics. Currently ranks by author-count only.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2764:++Outbox tracker state is *not* persisted to cache adapters — there is a `TODO` in `tracker.ts:48-49` noting rehydration is unimplemented. On every fresh app boot, the tracker is empty and the first wave of subscriptions pays full discovery cost.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3164:+5. TODO/FIXME/unimplemented sneaking into prose or code?
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3299:+/bin/zsh -lc 'git diff --name-only edc17b0~..51120cb | xargs rg -n "TODO|FIXME|unimplemented|TBD-from-research|pending agent landing|proposed|M_n|NIP-XX"' in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform

docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3322:+docs/design/podcast/copy.md:158:- `grep -RnE '// MARK: NMP-WIRE — TODO' ios/NmpPodcast/Views/` is part of the M11 exit gate (must be zero).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3323:+docs/design/podcast/copy.md:167:// MARK: NMP-WIRE — TODO
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3324:+docs/design/podcast/copy.md:281:- **`// MARK: NMP-WIRE — TODO` blocks are the only allowed change** to a view file before it is wired. Any other source change requires an explicit ADR + a parity-screenshots.md note with reviewer sign-off.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3325:+docs/design/podcast/copy.md:288:- `grep -RnE '// MARK: NMP-WIRE — TODO' ios/NmpPodcast/Views/ | wc -l` is the work-remaining counter for Step 7.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3327:+docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:61:+- **Fixture proving the kernel boundary** in `crates/fixture-todo-core` (~304 LOC) plus generated `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture`: a non-Nostr TODO module implementing all five substrate trait families, with codegen producing the per-app crate. Proves the kernel works for arbitrary domains, not just Nostr.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:117:+**Demo product:** `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture` — a TODO list app driven by the kernel substrate with no Nostr concepts in it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:240:-- **1a.1** **Kernel substrate prototype + non-Nostr fixture** — the five extension trait families (`DomainModule`, `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `CapabilityModule`, `IdentityModule`); composite reverse index; delta buffer with coalescing; claim-based GC; codegen producing a working `nmp-app-fixture` crate from a `fixture-todo-core` app module that implements all five trait families; desktop iced shell rendering a TODO list. ~2 weeks. **Proves: kernel substrate works for non-Nostr-shaped data; codegen pipeline functional.**
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:1008:Review for: (1) doctrine compliance, (2) TODO/FIXME/unimplemented sneaking in, (3) file-size budget, (4) internal consistency with the plan's own stated scope. Be terse. If everything is fine, say so. If there is a real concern, name the file:line and what to fix.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:316:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f1e374b.md:641:TODO/FIXME/unimplemented: none found.  
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:318:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f1e374b.md:652:TODO/FIXME/unimplemented: none found.  
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:324:docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:88:- **Two fixture apps prove the boundary in v1.** A tiny non-Nostr-shaped fixture (e.g., a TODO/notes module with an app-local identity) lands in 1a.1; the Twitter clone is the first Nostr-shaped extension module. Together they prove the kernel works in both directions.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:368:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:189:+- `selectOptimalRelays` (`helpers/relay-selection.ts:14-93`) is a greedy set-cover: each iteration picks the relay covering the most uncovered users, optionally biased by `score(relay, coverage, popularity)`. `maxRelaysPerUser` caps per-user concentration. The TODO at line 89 acknowledges that the final per-user relay list may exceed `maxRelaysPerUser` because the count is enforced during selection, not after.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:389:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:584:+The TODO at line 89 acknowledges a known imprecision: `maxRelaysPerUser` is enforced during the greedy pass but not as a post-filter, so a user can end up with more than `maxRelaysPerUser` final relays if their first picks happened to cover the pool.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:418:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1200:+Source explicitly notes the gap: `// TODO: The state of this tracker needs to be added to cache adapters` (`tracker.ts:48-49`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:419:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1230:+`calculateRelaySetFromEvent` doesn't fully account for relays where specific hashtags are popular (`relay/sets/calculate.ts:18-19` TODO). For tag-heavy posts (e.g., #bitdevs), publishing is suboptimal.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:420:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1234:+`getTopRelaysForAuthors` has TODO to incorporate quality metrics. Currently ranks by author-count only.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:442:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1480:+Outbox tracker state is *not* persisted to cache adapters — there is a `TODO` in `tracker.ts:48-49` noting rehydration is unimplemented. On every fresh app boot, the tracker is empty and the first wave of subscriptions pays full discovery cost.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:450:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1875:Review: (1) are the research findings internally consistent with NMP doctrine, (2) any TODO/FIXME, (3) file sizes, (4) any finding that contradicts the current plan or design. Be terse.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:828:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:222:./docs/research/ndk/missing-features-for-nmp.md:66:`calculateRelaySetFromEvent` doesn't fully account for relays where specific hashtags are popular (`relay/sets/calculate.ts:18-19` TODO). For tag-heavy posts (e.g., #bitdevs), publishing is suboptimal.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:829:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:223:./docs/research/ndk/missing-features-for-nmp.md:70:`getTopRelaysForAuthors` has TODO to incorporate quality metrics. Currently ranks by author-count only.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:830:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:224:./docs/research/ndk/outbox.md:98:Outbox tracker state is *not* persisted to cache adapters — there is a `TODO` in `tracker.ts:48-49` noting rehydration is unimplemented. On every fresh app boot, the tracker is empty and the first wave of subscriptions pays full discovery cost.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:831:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:225:./docs/research/applesauce/event-store-query-builders.md:138:- `selectOptimalRelays` (`helpers/relay-selection.ts:14-93`) is a greedy set-cover: each iteration picks the relay covering the most uncovered users, optionally biased by `score(relay, coverage, popularity)`. `maxRelaysPerUser` caps per-user concentration. The TODO at line 89 acknowledges that the final per-user relay list may exceed `maxRelaysPerUser` because the count is enforced during selection, not after.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:832:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:226:./docs/research/applesauce/outbox.md:84:The TODO at line 89 acknowledges a known imprecision: `maxRelaysPerUser` is enforced during the greedy pass but not as a post-filter, so a user can end up with more than `maxRelaysPerUser` final relays if their first picks happened to cover the pool.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:833:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:227:./docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:88:- **Two fixture apps prove the boundary in v1.** A tiny non-Nostr-shaped fixture (e.g., a TODO/notes module with an app-local identity) lands in 1a.1; the Twitter clone is the first Nostr-shaped extension module. Together they prove the kernel works in both directions.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:834:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:228:./docs/plan/m0-fixture.md:5:**Demo product:** `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture` — a TODO list app driven by the kernel substrate with no Nostr concepts in it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:835:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:229:./docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:483:1. Phase 1a.1 (kernel substrate prototype) ships with one fixture module (`fixture-todo-core`) demonstrating each of the five trait families. Codegen produces a working `nmp-app-fixture` crate. Desktop iced app renders a TODO list, no business logic in Swift / iced.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:840:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:234:./docs/design/podcast/copy.md:281:- **`// MARK: NMP-WIRE — TODO` blocks are the only allowed change** to a view file before it is wired. docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:850:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:244:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:603:+**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:882:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:276:./docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:7:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:883:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:277:./docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:25:- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:904:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:298:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1778:docs/plan.md:363:- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.
