docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:925:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:319:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:189:+- `selectOptimalRelays` (`helpers/relay-selection.ts:14-93`) is a greedy set-cover: each iteration picks the relay covering the most uncovered users, optionally biased by `score(relay, coverage, popularity)`. `maxRelaysPerUser` caps per-user concentration. The TODO at line 89 acknowledges that the final per-user relay list may exceed `maxRelaysPerUser` because the count is enforced during selection, not after.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:926:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:320:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:584:+The TODO at line 89 acknowledges a known imprecision: `maxRelaysPerUser` is enforced during the greedy pass but not as a post-filter, so a user can end up with more than `maxRelaysPerUser` final relays if their first picks happened to cover the pool.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:927:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:321:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1200:+Source explicitly notes the gap: `// TODO: The state of this tracker needs to be added to cache adapters` (`tracker.ts:48-49`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:928:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:322:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1230:+`calculateRelaySetFromEvent` doesn't fully account for relays where specific hashtags are popular (`relay/sets/calculate.ts:18-19` TODO). For tag-heavy posts (e.g., #bitdevs), publishing is suboptimal.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:929:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:323:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1234:+`getTopRelaysForAuthors` has TODO to incorporate quality metrics. Currently ranks by author-count only.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:930:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:324:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1480:+Outbox tracker state is *not* persisted to cache adapters — there is a `TODO` in `tracker.ts:48-49` noting rehydration is unimplemented. On every fresh app boot, the tracker is empty and the first wave of subscriptions pays full discovery cost.

Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:984:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:378:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2386:++**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:985:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:379:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2404:++- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Each pre-existing one is either resolved or has an ADR justifying the deferral with a tracking issue.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1010:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:404:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3161:-- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Each pre-existing one is either resolved or has an ADR justifying the deferral with a tracking issue.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1011:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:405:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3162:+- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1012:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:406:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3176:Review for: (1) doctrine compliance, (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented sneaking in, (3) test coverage where tests should exist, (4) file-size compliance (any new file > 300 LOC?), (5) docs internal consistency (M10.5 gate, M11 pod-NIP language, etc), (6) hidden shortcuts. Be terse. If fine, say so. If concern, name file:line + fix.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1036:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:430:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3481:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:852:docs/plan.md:363:- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Each pre-existing one is either resolved or has an ADR justifying the deferral with a tracking issue.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1064:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:458:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/151c80c.md:557:docs/design/podcast/copy.md:158:- `grep -RnE '// MARK: NMP-WIRE — TODO' ios/NmpPodcast/Views/` is part of the M11 exit gate (must be zero).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1065:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:459:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/151c80c.md:558:docs/design/podcast/copy.md:167:// MARK: NMP-WIRE — TODO
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1066:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:460:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/151c80c.md:559:docs/design/podcast/copy.md:281:- **`// MARK: NMP-WIRE — TODO` blocks are the only allowed change** to a view file before it is wired. Any other source change requires an explicit ADR + a parity-screenshots.md note with reviewer sign-off.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1067:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:461:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/151c80c.md:560:docs/design/podcast/copy.md:288:- `grep -RnE '// MARK: NMP-WIRE — TODO' ios/NmpPodcast/Views/ | wc -l` is the work-remaining counter for Step 7.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1068:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:462:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:61:+- **Fixture proving the kernel boundary** in `crates/fixture-todo-core` (~304 LOC) plus generated `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture`: a non-Nostr TODO module implementing all five substrate trait families, with codegen producing the per-app crate. Proves the kernel works for arbitrary domains, not just Nostr.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1069:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:463:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:117:+**Demo product:** `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture` — a TODO list app driven by the kernel substrate with no Nostr concepts in it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1070:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:464:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:240:-- **1a.1** **Kernel substrate prototype + non-Nostr fixture** — the five extension trait families (`DomainModule`, `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `CapabilityModule`, `IdentityModule`); composite reverse index; delta buffer with coalescing; claim-based GC; codegen producing a working `nmp-app-fixture` crate from a `fixture-todo-core` app module that implements all five trait families; desktop iced shell rendering a TODO list. ~2 weeks. **Proves: kernel substrate works for non-Nostr-shaped data; codegen pipeline functional.**

Be terse. If everything is fine, say so. If there is a real concern, name the file:line and what to fix.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1138:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:532:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6090:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2631:+docs/plan.md:21:- **Fixture proving the kernel boundary** in `crates/fixture-todo-core` (~304 LOC) plus generated `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture`: a non-Nostr TODO module implementing all five substrate trait families, with codegen producing the per-app crate. Proves the kernel works for arbitrary domains, not just Nostr.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1139:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:533:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6091:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2633:+docs/plan.md:77:**Demo product:** `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture` — a TODO list app driven by the kernel substrate with no Nostr concepts in it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1140:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:534:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6092:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2635:+docs/plan.md:345:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1141:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:535:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6093:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2637:+docs/plan.md:363:- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Each pre-existing one is either resolved or has an ADR justifying the deferral with a tracking issue.

All 20 code inspection findings are either justified by design or actionable as documentation cleanup. All cardinal doctrines (D0–D5) are upheld.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1158:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:552:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6122:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3161:-- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Each pre-existing one is either resolved or has an ADR justifying the deferral with a tracking issue.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1159:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:553:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6123:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3162:+- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1160:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:554:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6124:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3176:Review for: (1) doctrine compliance, (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented sneaking in, (3) test coverage where tests should exist, (4) file-size compliance (any new file > 300 LOC?), (5) docs internal consistency (M10.5 gate, M11 pod-NIP language, etc), (6) hidden shortcuts. Be terse. If fine, say so. If concern, name file:line + fix.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1182:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:576:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6167:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3478:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:848:docs/plan.md:77:**Demo product:** `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture` — a TODO list app driven by the kernel substrate with no Nostr concepts in it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1183:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:577:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6168:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3480:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:850:docs/plan.md:345:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1184:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:578:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6169:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3481:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:852:docs/plan.md:363:- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1240:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:634:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6545:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:601:+**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1241:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:635:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6547:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:619:+- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Each pre-existing one is either resolved or has an ADR justifying the deferral with a tracking issue.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1272:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:666:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:8173:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1275:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:669:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1405:+- `selectOptimalRelays` (`helpers/relay-selection.ts:14-93`) is a greedy set-cover: each iteration picks the relay covering the most uncovered users, optionally biased by `score(relay, coverage, popularity)`. `maxRelaysPerUser` caps per-user concentration. The TODO at line 89 acknowledges that the final per-user relay list may exceed `maxRelaysPerUser` because the count is enforced during selection, not after.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1276:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:670:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1800:+The TODO at line 89 acknowledges a known imprecision: `maxRelaysPerUser` is enforced during the greedy pass but not as a post-filter, so a user can end up with more than `maxRelaysPerUser` final relays if their first picks happened to cover the pool.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1277:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:671:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2416:+Source explicitly notes the gap: `// TODO: The state of this tracker needs to be added to cache adapters` (`tracker.ts:48-49`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1278:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:672:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2446:+`calculateRelaySetFromEvent` doesn't fully account for relays where specific hashtags are popular (`relay/sets/calculate.ts:18-19` TODO). For tag-heavy posts (e.g., #bitdevs), publishing is suboptimal.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1279:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:673:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2450:+`getTopRelaysForAuthors` has TODO to incorporate quality metrics. Currently ranks by author-count only.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1280:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:674:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2696:+Outbox tracker state is *not* persisted to cache adapters — there is a `TODO` in `tracker.ts:48-49` noting rehydration is unimplemented. On every fresh app boot, the tracker is empty and the first wave of subscriptions pays full discovery cost.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1288:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:682:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3259:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1289:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:683:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3262:docs/research/applesauce/event-store-query-builders.md:138:- `selectOptimalRelays` (`helpers/relay-selection.ts:14-93`) is a greedy set-cover: each iteration picks the relay covering the most uncovered users, optionally biased by `score(relay, coverage, popularity)`. `maxRelaysPerUser` caps per-user concentration. The TODO at line 89 acknowledges that the final per-user relay list may exceed `maxRelaysPerUser` because the count is enforced during selection, not after.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1290:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:684:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3263:docs/research/ndk/outbox.md:98:Outbox tracker state is *not* persisted to cache adapters — there is a `TODO` in `tracker.ts:48-49` noting rehydration is unimplemented. On every fresh app boot, the tracker is empty and the first wave of subscriptions pays full discovery cost.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1291:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:685:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3264:docs/research/ndk/missing-features-for-nmp.md:36:Source explicitly notes the gap: `// TODO: The state of this tracker needs to be added to cache adapters` (`tracker.ts:48-49`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1292:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:686:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3265:docs/research/ndk/missing-features-for-nmp.md:66:`calculateRelaySetFromEvent` doesn't fully account for relays where specific hashtags are popular (`relay/sets/calculate.ts:18-19` TODO). For tag-heavy posts (e.g., #bitdevs), publishing is suboptimal.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1293:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:687:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3266:docs/research/ndk/missing-features-for-nmp.md:70:`getTopRelaysForAuthors` has TODO to incorporate quality metrics. Currently ranks by author-count only.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1294:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:688:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3267:docs/research/applesauce/outbox.md:84:The TODO at line 89 acknowledges a known imprecision: `maxRelaysPerUser` is enforced during the greedy pass but not as a post-filter, so a user can end up with more than `maxRelaysPerUser` final relays if their first picks happened to cover the pool.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1295:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:689:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:5557:   138	- `selectOptimalRelays` (`helpers/relay-selection.ts:14-93`) is a greedy set-cover: each iteration picks the relay covering the most uncovered users, optionally biased by `score(relay, coverage, popularity)`. `maxRelaysPerUser` caps per-user concentration. The TODO at line 89 acknowledges that the final per-user relay list may exceed `maxRelaysPerUser` because the count is enforced during selection, not after.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1306:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:700:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3055:docs/plan.md:345:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1333:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:727:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1419:docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory.md:396:The FFI and iOS bridge code is **clean of blocking debt markers** (TODO/FIXME/unimplemented/panic). All 20 code inspection findings are either justified by design or actionable as documentation cleanup. All cardinal doctrines (D0–D5) are upheld.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1334:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:728:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1429:docs/plan.md:21:- **Fixture proving the kernel boundary** in `crates/fixture-todo-core` (~304 LOC) plus generated `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture`: a non-Nostr TODO module implementing all five substrate trait families, with codegen producing the per-app crate. Proves the kernel works for arbitrary domains, not just Nostr.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1335:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:729:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1433:docs/plan.md:77:**Demo product:** `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture` — a TODO list app driven by the kernel substrate with no Nostr concepts in it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1336:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:730:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1440:docs/plan.md:345:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1337:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:731:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1442:docs/plan.md:363:- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1338:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:732:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1451:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1567:Review for: (1) doctrine compliance (in particular D0 — does nmp-nip65 propose any kernel-side type leaks? D4 — single source for relay-set facts?); (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented; (3) file-size compliance (any new file > 300 LOC?); (4) internal consistency across the 8 sub-docs; (5) compatibility with the upcoming M3 LMDB schema (e.g. watermark table); (6) the planned test (m2_subscription_compilation_audit) exit criteria; (7) any hidden shortcuts. Be terse; name file:line + fix for any concern.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1339:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:733:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1455:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1621:/bin/zsh -lc 'rg -n "TODO|FIXME|unimplemented|panic!|todo!|unwrap'"\\(|TBD|stub|placeholder|does not exist yet|proposed|deferred|open question|future|post-M2|M6|M3|M4|M5|M8|M9\" docs/design/subscription-compilation.md docs/design/subscription-compilation" in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1340:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:734:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1473:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1778:docs/plan.md:363:- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1341:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:735:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1476:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1940:docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:88:- **Two fixture apps prove the boundary in v1.** A tiny non-Nostr-shaped fixture (e.g., a TODO/notes module with an app-local identity) lands in 1a.1; the Twitter clone is the first Nostr-shaped extension module. Together they prove the kernel works in both directions.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1384:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:778:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1675:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3423:docs/plan.md:21:- **Fixture proving the kernel boundary** in `crates/fixture-todo-core` (~304 LOC) plus generated `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture`: a non-Nostr TODO module implementing all five substrate trait families, with codegen producing the per-app crate. Proves the kernel works for arbitrary domains, not just Nostr.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1385:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:779:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1676:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3424:docs/plan.md:77:**Demo product:** `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture` — a TODO list app driven by the kernel substrate with no Nostr concepts in it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1386:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:780:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1677:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3426:docs/plan.md:345:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1387:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:781:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1678:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3427:docs/plan.md:363:- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.

Be terse; name file:line + fix for any concern.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1434:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:828:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1984:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:2979:7. Any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented sneaking in?
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1435:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:829:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1988:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3055:docs/plan.md:345:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1497:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:891:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:1838:docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:25:- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:1511:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:905:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1552:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

Desktop iced app renders a TODO list, no business logic in Swift / iced.

Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2896:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1419:docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory.md:396:The FFI and iOS bridge code is **clean of blocking debt markers** (TODO/FIXME/unimplemented/panic). All 20 code inspection findings are either justified by design or actionable as documentation cleanup. All cardinal doctrines (D0–D5) are upheld.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2899:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1429:docs/plan.md:21:- **Fixture proving the kernel boundary** in `crates/fixture-todo-core` (~304 LOC) plus generated `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture`: a non-Nostr TODO module implementing all five substrate trait families, with codegen producing the per-app crate. Proves the kernel works for arbitrary domains, not just Nostr.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2902:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1433:docs/plan.md:77:**Demo product:** `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture` — a TODO list app driven by the kernel substrate with no Nostr concepts in it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2906:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1440:docs/plan.md:345:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2908:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1442:docs/plan.md:363:- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2913:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1451:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1567:Review for: (1) doctrine compliance (in particular D0 — does nmp-nip65 propose any kernel-side type leaks? D4 — single source for relay-set facts?); (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented; (3) file-size compliance (any new file > 300 LOC?); (4) internal consistency across the 8 sub-docs; (5) compatibility with the upcoming M3 LMDB schema (e.g. watermark table); (6) the planned test (m2_subscription_compilation_audit) exit criteria; (7) any hidden shortcuts. Be terse; name file:line + fix for any concern.

All 20 code inspection findings are either justified by design or actionable as documentation cleanup. All cardinal doctrines (D0–D5) are upheld.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3072:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1648:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3161:-- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Each pre-existing one is either resolved or has an ADR justifying the deferral with a tracking issue.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3073:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1649:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3162:+- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3074:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1650:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3176:Review for: (1) doctrine compliance, (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented sneaking in, (3) test coverage where tests should exist, (4) file-size compliance (any new file > 300 LOC?), (5) docs internal consistency (M10.5 gate, M11 pod-NIP language, etc), (6) hidden shortcuts. Be terse. If fine, say so. If concern, name file:line + fix.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3096:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1675:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3423:docs/plan.md:21:- **Fixture proving the kernel boundary** in `crates/fixture-todo-core` (~304 LOC) plus generated `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture`: a non-Nostr TODO module implementing all five substrate trait families, with codegen producing the per-app crate. Proves the kernel works for arbitrary domains, not just Nostr.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3097:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1676:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3424:docs/plan.md:77:**Demo product:** `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture` — a TODO list app driven by the kernel substrate with no Nostr concepts in it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3098:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1677:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3426:docs/plan.md:345:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3099:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1678:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3427:docs/plan.md:363:- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3278:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1951:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1388:Review: (1) doctrine compliance (in particular D0 + D2 — composite reverse index + working-set bound must remain enforced through the trait); (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented; (3) file-size compliance; (4) consistency of EventStore trait surface across the 6 sub-docs; (5) the choice of nostr-lmdb as on-disk byte store vs roll-our-own — is the gap analysis honest?; (6) the GC eviction algorithm + budget; (7) the migration plumbing's testability; (8) any hidden shortcuts. Be terse; name file:line + fix for any concern.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3306:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1988:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3055:docs/plan.md:345:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3604:docs/research/applesauce/event-store-query-builders.md:138:- `selectOptimalRelays` (`helpers/relay-selection.ts:14-93`) is a greedy set-cover: each iteration picks the relay covering the most uncovered users, optionally biased by `score(relay, coverage, popularity)`. `maxRelaysPerUser` caps per-user concentration. The TODO at line 89 acknowledges that the final per-user relay list may exceed `maxRelaysPerUser` because the count is enforced during selection, not after.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3607:docs/design/podcast/wiring.md:11:3. Replace `// MARK: NMP-WIRE — TODO` blocks with `// MARK: NMP-WIRE — wired` consuming the generated `@<View>` property wrapper.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3626:docs/research/applesauce/outbox.md:84:The TODO at line 89 acknowledges a known imprecision: `maxRelaysPerUser` is enforced during the greedy pass but not as a post-filter, so a user can end up with more than `maxRelaysPerUser` final relays if their first picks happened to cover the pool.

commit 446a2120a2adde3318a4b19fc13650dc34def8dc
Author: pablof7z <pfer@me.com>
Date:   Mon May 18 02:06:43 2026 +0300

    docs(m11): fix risks.md §A.2 rig.rs scope conflict
    
    §A.2 said rig.rs divergence is theoretical until M15; contradicts
    podcast-llm.md §K and exit-gate.md which require rig.rs to land and
    be CI-tested in M11. Updated to reflect that the rig.rs path is
    demonstrated in M11 (not deferred), while multi-platform UX divergence
    analysis remains deferred to docs/perf/m15/.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

diff --git a/docs/design/podcast/risks.md b/docs/design/podcast/risks.md
index 888cc2d..a4a827f 100644
--- a/docs/design/podcast/risks.md
+++ b/docs/design/podcast/risks.md
@@ -5,41 +5,41 @@
 
 ---
 
 ## A. Risks (high → low)
 
 ### A.1 sqlite-vec on iOS — static linking with `load_extension`
 
 - **Severity**: High. Top risk in the whole M11 stack.
 - **Why**: `rusqlite` with `bundled` compiles `libsqlite3` from source, which is fine. Loading `vec0` at runtime requires `SQLITE_ENABLE_LOAD_EXTENSION`. iOS App Store review historically rejects `dlopen` of arbitrary dylibs; the safer path is **static link** of the vec0 symbols into the libsqlite3 we build, behind `SQLITE_CORE`.
 - **Mitigation**:
   1. Spike before Step 1C: build a minimal Rust+rusqlite+sqlite-vec crate, archive for `aarch64-apple-ios`, embed in a throwaway xcframework, install on iPhone 12, run a 1000-vector insert + search round-trip.
   2. Document the `build.rs` patch (cflags + statically registered extension) in `docs/design/podcast/podcast-rag.md`.
   3. **Fallback**: if static link is infeasible, ship a Rust-side `instant-distance` HNSW backend behind the same `VectorStore` trait. Schema persists to a flat `.bin` file instead of SQLite. Worse cold-start but unblocks M11.
 - **Owner**: assigned at M11 kickoff; spike result is a blocker for `podcast-rag` impl beginning.
 
 ### A.2 Apple Intelligence is iOS-only → cross-platform divergence
 
 - **Severity**: Medium-High.
 - **Why**: The reference app's entire LLM surface uses `FoundationModels.LanguageModelSession`. On iOS we route through `AppleIntelligenceCapability` (free, on-device). On Android/Desktop/Web (post-M11) we must route through `rig.rs`, which means: a provider, an API key, network calls, cost. The UX divergence is real — Ask becomes a fundamentally different feature off-iOS.
 - **Mitigation**:
-  1. M11 ships iOS only — divergence is theoretical until M15.
+  1. M11 ships iOS only **and** requires the rig.rs path to land and be CI-tested against a real LLM endpoint (see `podcast-llm.md` §K). Divergence across platforms is real post-M11, but the rig.rs path is demonstrated in M11, not deferred to M15.
   2. The router lives in Rust (`podcast-llm/src/router.rs`) so the policy is centrally testable.
   3. Document divergence + behavioral expectations in `docs/perf/m15/cross-platform.md` (deferred).
   4. Settings surface (post-M11) lets the user pick "Local (Apple)" / "OpenAI" / "Anthropic" / "Local (Ollama URL)" — same code path, different routing.
 
 ### A.3 Foundation Models prompt-shape parity vs `rig.rs`
 
 - **Severity**: Medium.
 - **Why**: The Foundation Models API takes one big string + optional system prompt; `rig.rs` likes a structured `Vec<ChatMessage>` with role tags. The same prompt routed two ways can produce divergent output if the framing changes ("User: ... Assistant: ..." in-string vs proper message roles).
 - **Mitigation**:
   1. `podcast-llm/src/prompts.rs` defines each prompt as both a flat string (for AppleIntel) AND a `Vec<rig::completion::Message>` (for rig). Both shapes are byte-deterministic functions of the same inputs.
   2. Golden tests: same input → both shapes → snapshotted; reviewed when changed.
   3. Where output format matters (the `CHAPTER|...|YES` parsing in `extractChapters`, the `FOUND|MM:SS|...` in `findRelevantTimestamp`), the parser is a single Rust function that handles both routes' outputs. Tests cover each route's raw output samples.
 
 ### A.4 Embedding-dimension swap mid-project
 
 - **Severity**: Medium.
 - **Why**: We choose `bge-small-en-v1.5` at 384 dims. Future requests for `bge-large-en-v1.5` (1024 dims) or `text-embedding-3-large` (3072 dims) require a sqlite-vec schema migration that rebuilds every existing index. Each embedding takes ~12 ms on iPhone 12; 5,000 chunks per podcast × 100 podcasts × 12 ms = 100 minutes of background re-index per device.
 - **Mitigation**:
   1. Lock 384 dims in M11. Schema records the model name.
   2. Migration is an explicit `RebuildEmbeddingIndex` action with progress UI.

The commit is contained by `master` and only changes `docs/design/podcast/risks.md`; the edited file is 141 LOC and the added line has no TODO/FIXME marker. I’m checking the referenced `podcast-llm.md` and exit gate claims now.
exec
/bin/zsh -lc "git show 446a212:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md | nl -ba | sed -n '1,260p'" in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/.claude/worktrees/agent-a8c0acab50f455ada
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# Step 1B — `apps/podcast/podcast-llm`
     2	
     3	> Parent: [`../podcast-app-rebuild.md`](../podcast-app-rebuild.md)
     4	> Reference Swift sources: `Services/AIService.swift` (308 LOC), `Services/GuestEnrichmentService.swift` (94 LOC), `Services/InsightService.swift` (233 LOC, the `matchExcerpt` part), `Views/Ask/AskView.swift` (322 LOC), `Views/Insights/InsightsView.swift` (252 LOC), `Views/Player/GuestAgentSheet.swift` (297 LOC), `Views/Player/ChaptersPanel.swift` (324 LOC — the `findRelevantTimestamp` integration).
     5	
     6	`podcast-llm` owns every LLM-driven action: ask-the-corpus (RAG-grounded chat), per-episode summarization, chapter extraction, "find this topic" timestamp search, guest enrichment, guest-agent chat, and excerpt matching. It is **dual-path**: Apple Intelligence on-device on iOS via a capability bridge; `rig.rs` cross-platform fallback.
     7	
     8	---
     9	
    10	## A. Why dual-path
    11	
    12	The reference Swift app uses `import FoundationModels` and `LanguageModelSession()`. That is Apple Intelligence on-device — free, private, requires no API key, but **iOS-only and capped to Apple-managed models**. To preserve UX parity on iPhone (no API key onboarding, no network for inference), the rebuild must route through Apple Intelligence when available.
    13	
    14	For Android/Desktop/Web (post-M11) the same actions route through `rig.rs` against a configured provider. The action shape is identical; the routing decision lives in `podcast-llm/src/router.rs` and reads from `SettingsRecord.llm_preferred_route`.
    15	
    16	The Foundation Models prompts in `AIService.swift` (summary, chapter-extract, find-relevant-timestamp, chat, embed-stub) are **byte-identical** in the Rust crate. Identical prompts to identical model = identical outputs. Any prompt change is an ADR.
    17	
    18	---
    19	
    20	## B. `Cargo.toml`
    21	
    22	```toml
    23	[package]
    24	name = "podcast-llm"
    25	version.workspace = true
    26	edition.workspace = true
    27	license.workspace = true
    28	
    29	[dependencies]
    30	nmp-core = { path = "../../../crates/nmp-core" }
    31	podcast-core = { path = "../podcast-core" }
    32	podcast-rag = { path = "../podcast-rag" }
    33	rig-core = "0.4"          # crate name on crates.io is `rig-core`; importable as `rig`
    34	serde = { workspace = true, features = ["derive"] }
    35	serde_json = { workspace = true }
    36	ulid = "1"
    37	url = "2"
    38	async-trait = "0.1"
    39	futures = "0.3"
    40	tracing = "0.1"
    41	thiserror = "1"
    42	
    43	[features]
    44	default = ["rig-openai", "rig-anthropic"]
    45	rig-openai = []
    46	rig-anthropic = []
    47	```
    48	
    49	`rig-core` is chosen over `langchain-rust` because: smaller dependency footprint; first-class streaming `StreamingChat` traits; multi-provider with a uniform `CompletionModel` trait; actively maintained; no Python interop. The trade-off is fewer prebuilt agents — we want lean orchestration, not framework lock-in.
    50	
    51	---
    52	
    53	## C. Router
    54	
    55	```rust
    56	pub enum LlmRoute {
    57	    AppleIntelligence,
    58	    RigProvider { provider: RigProvider, model: String },
    59	}
    60	
    61	pub enum RigProvider {
    62	    OpenAI,
    63	    Anthropic,
    64	    Local { base_url: Url },   // model comes from LlmRoute::RigProvider.model
    65	}
    66	
    67	pub fn select_route(settings: &SettingsRecord, capabilities: &CapabilityAvailability) -> LlmRoute {
    68	    match settings.llm_preferred_route {
    69	        Some(LlmRoutePref::AppleIntelligence) if capabilities.apple_intelligence => LlmRoute::AppleIntelligence,
    70	        Some(LlmRoutePref::Rig { provider, model }) => LlmRoute::RigProvider { provider, model },
    71	        _ if capabilities.apple_intelligence => LlmRoute::AppleIntelligence,
    72	        _ => LlmRoute::RigProvider { provider: RigProvider::OpenAI, model: "gpt-4o-mini".into() },
    73	    }
    74	}
    75	```
    76	
    77	The actor injects `CapabilityAvailability` at boot (probed by the bridge); subsequent capability state changes invalidate cached availability.
    78	
    79	---
    80	
    81	## D. Action modules
    82	
    83	### D.1 `AskQuestion`
    84	
    85	```rust
    86	pub struct AskQuestion {
    87	    pub session_id: ChatSessionId,    // ulid; owns chat history
    88	    pub query: String,
    89	    pub rag_filter: Option<RagFilter>,  // RagFilter from podcast-rag (episode_ids, podcast_ids)
    90	}
    91	
    92	pub enum AskQuestionOutput { Started { token_stream_id: Ulid } }
    93	```
    94	
    95	Step machine:
    96	
    97	1. `Validating` — non-empty query, session exists.
    98	2. `Retrieving` — `ctx.dispatch(PodcastRag::Retrieve { query, limit: 5, filter })`.
    99	3. `Generating` — assemble prompt (per `AIService.chat` system prompt; cites `[1]`..`[N]`). On `AppleIntelligence` route: `AwaitCapability(AppleIntelligenceCapability::StreamGenerate)`. On `rig` route: spawn a Rust-side streaming task; tokens come back as `InternalEvent::LlmToken`.
   100	4. As each token arrives: append to the in-memory `ChatSessionState` (lives in `podcast-llm::sessions` projection cache, not in `AppState`); emit a `AskViewModule::Delta::TokenAppended { session_id, token }` (≤ 30 deltas/sec coalesced per ADR-0002).
   101	5. `Complete` when stream finishes: write final `ChatTurn` with source citations to the session.
   102	
   103	Streaming contract: tokens are bundled into the `ViewBatch` lane (typed deltas), not the `SideEffect` lane. The Swift side reads `ChatTurnPayload { content_so_far: String, sources: Vec<SourceChip>, is_streaming: bool }` and re-renders incrementally — same UI shape as `MessageBubble` in `AskView.swift`.
   104	
   105	### D.2 `RunInsight`
   106	
   107	The reference app calls this implicitly: the `InsightsView` is just a list; *creating* an insight is the `StopInsightRecording` action chain that ends in `MatchExcerpt`. `RunInsight` here is the **on-demand episode insight** generation — a structured episode-summary action distinct from the `SummarizeEpisode` brief/detailed summary. M11 ships it because the M11 exit gate mentions "Insights view generates a structured episode summary on demand."
   108	
   109	```rust
   110	pub struct RunInsight { pub episode_id: EpisodeId }
   111	pub enum RunInsightOutput { Generated { sections: Vec<InsightSectionPayload> } }
   112	```
   113	
   114	Prompt: a structured-output prompt that asks for `key_points: [String]`, `quotes: [{ speaker?: String, text: String, timestamp_s?: f64 }]`, `topics: [String]`. Routes via `LlmRoute`.
   115	
   116	### D.3 `EnrichGuest`
   117	
   118	```rust
   119	pub struct EnrichGuest { pub guest_id: GuestId }
   120	pub enum EnrichGuestOutput { Enriched { bio: String, content_chunks_indexed: u32 } }
   121	```
   122	
   123	Step machine matches `GuestEnrichmentService.swift`:
   124	
   125	1. Walk up to 3 most-recent appearances → extract mention sentences.
   126	2. Build context prompt → generate bio → write `GuestRecord.bio`, `last_enriched_at_ms`.
   127	3. Optionally fetch external sources (Twitter handle / website) via `HttpCapability` and `GuestContent::ingest`.
   128	
   129	### D.4 `AskGuest`
   130	
   131	```rust
   132	pub struct AskGuest { pub guest_id: GuestId, pub episode_id: EpisodeId, pub query: String }
   133	```
   134	
   135	Reuses the `AskQuestion` machine with a guest-specific system prompt and `RagFilter::ByGuest(guest_id)`.
   136	
   137	### D.5 `FindRelevantTimestamp`
   138	
   139	```rust
   140	pub struct FindRelevantTimestamp { pub episode_id: EpisodeId, pub query: String }
   141	pub enum FindRelevantTimestampOutput {
   142	    Found { timestamp_s: f64, context: String },
   143	    NotFound,
   144	}
   145	```
   146	
   147	Matches `AIService.findRelevantTimestamp` byte-equal. Used by `ChaptersPanel.swift`'s search bar.
   148	
   149	### D.6 `MatchExcerpt`
   150	
   151	```rust
   152	pub struct MatchExcerpt {
   153	    pub episode_id: EpisodeId,
   154	    pub thought_text: String,
   155	    pub capture_time_s: f64,
   156	}
   157	pub enum MatchExcerptOutput { Matched { text: String, start_s: f64, end_s: f64 } }
   158	```
   159	
   160	10-minute window before capture_time_s → AI matches the relevant excerpt. Called inside `StopInsightRecording`'s step machine.
   161	
   162	### D.7 `SummarizeEpisode` (delegated by `podcast-core::SummarizeEpisode`)
   163	
   164	`podcast-core` owns the action **shape**; the LLM call is delegated to `podcast-llm` via a private `ctx.dispatch_internal(SummarizePrompt { episode_id, style })` (one actor message tick — no FFI boundary crossed). This avoids `podcast-core` depending on `podcast-llm` while keeping the action namespaced under `podcast.*` from the Swift app's POV.
   165	
   166	---
   167	
   168	## E. ViewModules
   169	
   170	### E.1 `AskViewModule`
   171	
   172	Single view module driving `AskView.swift`. Reads from `podcast-llm::sessions::ChatSessionState`:
   173	
   174	```rust
   175	pub struct AskView {
   176	    pub session_id: ChatSessionId,
   177	    pub messages: Vec<ChatTurnPayload>,
   178	    pub suggested: Vec<String>,          // pre-formatted suggestion chips
   179	    pub episode_count: u32,
   180	    pub hours_listened_str: String,      // pre-formatted "12 hours listened"
   181	    pub is_loading: bool,
   182	}
   183	
   184	pub struct ChatTurnPayload {
   185	    pub id: Ulid,
   186	    pub role: ChatRole,                  // User | Assistant
   187	    pub content: String,                 // already grown by streaming token deltas
   188	    pub sources: Vec<SourceChipPayload>,
   189	    pub is_streaming: bool,
   190	}
   191	
   192	pub struct SourceChipPayload {
   193	    pub episode_title: String,
   194	    pub podcast_title: String,
   195	    pub timestamp_str: Option<String>,   // "12:34" pre-formatted
   196	    pub is_insight: bool,
   197	}
   198	```
   199	
   200	### E.2 `GuestAgentViewModule`
   201	
   202	Drives `GuestAgentSheet.swift`. Specced by `{ guest_id, episode_id }`.
   203	
   204	---
   205	
   206	## F. Prompt library
   207	
   208	Lives in `podcast-llm/src/prompts.rs`. **Byte-identical** to the Swift source. Each prompt is a `const &str` plus a builder function for parameter injection:
   209	
   210	```rust
   211	pub const SUMMARY_SYSTEM: &str = "..."; // empty in reference; user message carries everything
   212	pub fn build_summary_user(text: &str, style: SummaryStyle) -> String { /* mirrors AIService.buildSummaryPrompt */ }
   213	
   214	pub fn build_chapter_extract_user(chunks: &[(String, f64, f64)]) -> String { /* mirrors AIService.extractChapters prompt */ }
   215	
   216	pub fn build_find_timestamp_user(query: &str, chunks: &[(String, f64, f64)]) -> String { /* … */ }
   217	
   218	pub fn build_chat_system(context: &str) -> String { /* RAGService.chat systemPrompt */ }
   219	```
   220	
   221	The 8 KB transcript truncation cap and 12 KB chapter-extract cap from the Swift source are preserved as `const usize`. Future tuning is an ADR.
   222	
   223	---
   224	
   225	## G. Streaming pipeline
   226	
   227	```
   228	AppleIntelligenceCapability::StreamGenerate { prompt }
   229	    → bridge emits SidePush::Token { stream_id, token }
   230	        each token: kernel → AskAction::reduce(AssistantTokenArrived)
   231	            ↳ append to ChatSessionState.streaming_buffer
   232	            ↳ emit AskViewModule::Delta::TokenAppended (coalesced per ADR-0002)
   233	                ↳ Swift @StateObject re-renders incrementally
   234	    → bridge emits SidePush::Finish { stream_id }
   235	        kernel → AskAction::reduce(StreamFinished)
   236	            ↳ commit final ChatTurn to ChatSession history
   237	            ↳ emit AskViewModule::Delta::TurnCommitted
   238	```
   239	
   240	Streaming-output back-pressure: if the coalescer drops tokens (≥60 Hz), they accumulate in `ChatSessionState.streaming_buffer` server-side; the next delta carries the full buffer. The Swift side reads `content` directly — no lost tokens.
   241	
   242	For `rig.rs` routes, the same shape: `rig::completion::StreamingChat` yields a `Stream<Item = String>`; an internal Rust task awaits each, converts to `InternalEvent::LlmToken`, posts to the actor.
   243	
   244	---
   245	
   246	## H. Cache strategy
   247	
   248	- **Per-session chat history**: in-memory `ChatSessionState`. Survives navigation. Drops 5 minutes after the session view module unsubscribes (per ADR-0005 warmth).
   249	- **Per-episode generated content** (summary, chapters, find-timestamp answers): persisted on `EpisodeRecord.ai_summary`, `ChapterRecord`s, and a `find_timestamp_cache: HashMap<(EpisodeId, query_hash), TimestampHit>` projection (size-capped at 100 entries per episode).
   250	- **Per-guest enrichment**: persisted on `GuestRecord.bio` + `GuestRecord.last_enriched_at_ms`. Stale after 30 days → silently re-enriched on next view open.
   251	- **No prompt response caching** for Ask. Each query is a fresh stream; the cost is borne by the user, and the prompt is dynamic (uses RAG context).
   252	
   253	---
   254	
   255	## I. API key storage
   256	
   257	`KeyValueStoreCapability` (see [`capabilities.md`](capabilities.md)) hosts:
   258	
   259	- `podcast.llm.openai_api_key`
   260	- `podcast.llm.anthropic_api_key`

446a212:docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:8:Six were named in `docs/plan.md` §M11; three (`TranscriptionCapability`, `VoiceRecordingCapability`, `AppleIntelligenceCapability`) are added here because parity demands them — without them the rebuild routes around the entire on-device Apple Intelligence + Speech stack that the reference app depends on.
446a212:docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:248:Non-iOS impl (post-M11): whisper.cpp via the `whisper-rs` crate or remote provider via `rig.rs`.
446a212:docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:277:## I. `AppleIntelligenceCapability`
446a212:docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:305:iOS impl: `FoundationModels.SystemLanguageModel.default` + `LanguageModelSession()` (per `AIService.swift`). `StreamGenerate` wraps `session.streamResponse(to:)` and emits `Token` events for each `chunk.content`. Bounded state: one `LanguageModelSession` per active stream id. Idempotent cancel.
446a212:docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:307:Non-iOS impl: **none** — the capability is iOS-only. On other platforms the actor reports `Unavailable { reason: DeviceNotSupported }`. The router (see [`podcast-llm.md`](podcast-llm.md) §C) routes via `rig` instead.
446a212:docs/design/podcast/exit-gate.md:32:| Ask a question via **rig.rs** against a real non-Apple-Intelligence LLM endpoint (M11 multiplatform proof); separately also via Apple Intelligence on-device | `docs/perf/m11/ask-streaming.md` with first-token-latency on both routes; rig.rs path is **required**, not optional — it is the proof that the architecture works off iOS | benchmarked: `just demo-ask-stream --route rig` AND `just demo-ask-stream --route apple-intelligence` both pass |
446a212:docs/design/podcast/inventory.md:45:| `Services/AIService.swift` | 308 | `SummarizeEpisode`, `ExtractChapters`, `FindRelevantTimestamp` actions + the prompt library | `podcast-llm` | `AppleIntelligenceCapability` (iOS) or `rig.rs` (fallback) |
446a212:docs/design/podcast/inventory.md:48:| `Services/GuestEnrichmentService.swift` | 94 | `IdentifyGuests`, `EnrichGuest` actions; uses transcript context | `podcast-llm` | `AppleIntelligenceCapability` |
446a212:docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:4:> Sibling crates: [`podcast-llm.md`](podcast-llm.md), [`podcast-rag.md`](podcast-rag.md), [`podcast-feeds.md`](podcast-feeds.md), [`capabilities.md`](capabilities.md).
446a212:docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:213:Not visible in `../podcast` today (the Swift app's `QueueView` is the processing queue, not a playback queue). M11 ships it **disabled by default** behind a Settings flag — present for the architecture proof but invisible until enabled. UI deferred.
446a212:docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:307:// AwaitCapability: AppleIntelligenceCapability::Generate { prompt, style } OR PodcastLlm route.
446a212:docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:310:// AwaitCapability: AppleIntelligenceCapability::Generate ; parses out the CHAPTER|... format ;
446a212:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:6:`podcast-llm` owns every LLM-driven action: ask-the-corpus (RAG-grounded chat), per-episode summarization, chapter extraction, "find this topic" timestamp search, guest enrichment, guest-agent chat, and excerpt matching. It is **dual-path**: Apple Intelligence on-device on iOS via a capability bridge; `rig.rs` cross-platform fallback.
446a212:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:12:The reference Swift app uses `import FoundationModels` and `LanguageModelSession()`. That is Apple Intelligence on-device — free, private, requires no API key, but **iOS-only and capped to Apple-managed models**. To preserve UX parity on iPhone (no API key onboarding, no network for inference), the rebuild must route through Apple Intelligence when available.
446a212:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:14:For Android/Desktop/Web (post-M11) the same actions route through `rig.rs` against a configured provider. The action shape is identical; the routing decision lives in `podcast-llm/src/router.rs` and reads from `SettingsRecord.llm_preferred_route`.
446a212:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:99:3. `Generating` — assemble prompt (per `AIService.chat` system prompt; cites `[1]`..`[N]`). On `AppleIntelligence` route: `AwaitCapability(AppleIntelligenceCapability::StreamGenerate)`. On `rig` route: spawn a Rust-side streaming task; tokens come back as `InternalEvent::LlmToken`.
446a212:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:107:The reference app calls this implicitly: the `InsightsView` is just a list; *creating* an insight is the `StopInsightRecording` action chain that ends in `MatchExcerpt`. `RunInsight` here is the **on-demand episode insight** generation — a structured episode-summary action distinct from the `SummarizeEpisode` brief/detailed summary. M11 ships it because the M11 exit gate mentions "Insights view generates a structured episode summary on demand."
446a212:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:228:AppleIntelligenceCapability::StreamGenerate { prompt }
446a212:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:242:For `rig.rs` routes, the same shape: `rig::completion::StreamingChat` yields a `Stream<Item = String>`; an internal Rust task awaits each, converts to `InternalEvent::LlmToken`, posts to the actor.
446a212:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:263:**M11 provider configuration.** Because rig.rs is required in M11 (see §K), the API key must be reachable in M11. In M11 the key is injected via environment variables at test time:
446a212:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:282:## K. rig.rs path — M11 required (not deferred)
446a212:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:284:The rig.rs route through `rig-core` is **required to land and pass its end-to-end test in M11**. It is not deferred to M15 or any later milestone. Rationale: M11 is the kernel-boundary check; the rig.rs path is the proof that `podcast-llm` is genuinely multiplatform — not an iOS wrapper around Apple Intelligence with a Rust veneer.
446a212:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:286:**M11 acceptance for rig.rs:**
446a212:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:293:Apple Intelligence remains the default iOS route. The rig.rs path is the non-Apple route that M11 must demonstrate. Both routes share the same action shape — the only difference is `select_route()`'s return value.
446a212:docs/design/podcast/risks.md:23:- **Why**: The reference app's entire LLM surface uses `FoundationModels.LanguageModelSession`. On iOS we route through `AppleIntelligenceCapability` (free, on-device). On Android/Desktop/Web (post-M11) we must route through `rig.rs`, which means: a provider, an API key, network calls, cost. The UX divergence is real — Ask becomes a fundamentally different feature off-iOS.
446a212:docs/design/podcast/risks.md:25:  1. M11 ships iOS only **and** requires the rig.rs path to land and be CI-tested against a real LLM endpoint (see `podcast-llm.md` §K). Divergence across platforms is real post-M11, but the rig.rs path is demonstrated in M11, not deferred to M15.
446a212:docs/design/podcast/risks.md:26:  2. The router lives in Rust (`podcast-llm/src/router.rs`) so the policy is centrally testable.
446a212:docs/design/podcast/risks.md:30:### A.3 Foundation Models prompt-shape parity vs `rig.rs`
446a212:docs/design/podcast/risks.md:33:- **Why**: The Foundation Models API takes one big string + optional system prompt; `rig.rs` likes a structured `Vec<ChatMessage>` with role tags. The same prompt routed two ways can produce divergent output if the framing changes ("User: ... Assistant: ..." in-string vs proper message roles).
446a212:docs/design/podcast/risks.md:135:- **Recommendation**: `ExportOpml` follows in M11 if Settings UI allows (the existing Settings has no Import/Export section; adding one is one-screen). Defer to a Settings ADR. M11 ships Import only.
446a212:docs/design/podcast/wiring.md:132:- Capability deps: `AppleIntelligenceCapability` (on iOS) or `rig.rs` provider with `KeyValueStoreCapability` for API keys.
446a212:docs/plan.md:402:| `Ask/` | AskView.swift | `ask-core` ActionModule + ViewModule wrapping `rig.rs` LLM call |
446a212:docs/plan.md:405:| `Insights/` | InsightsView | `insights-core` ViewModule + ActionModule (uses RAG via `rig.rs`) |
446a212:docs/plan.md:429:- `apps/podcast/podcast-llm/` — LLM-driven actions via `rig.rs`: `AskQuestion`, `EnrichGuest`, `RunInsight`. Uses the kernel's capability bridge for HTTP + key storage.
446a212:docs/plan.md:468:- **Ask a question** about an episode; answer streams in via `rig.rs` LLM with the transcript as RAG context.
446a212:docs/plan.md:559:### M15 — Cross-platform: Android + Desktop + Web
446a212:docs/plan.md:667:| 07 | nmp-nip07 | M15 | web-only browser signer |
446a212:docs/plan.md:680:| 55 | nmp-nip55 | M15 | Android Amber bridge |
446a212:docs/plan.md:702:- **M15 (Android + Desktop + Web)** is three parallel tracks once M14 (UniFFI) lands.
446a212:docs/plan.md:726:The cross-platform consistency tests are the highest-value tier post-M15.
446a212:docs/plan.md:741:- Android build from M15.
446a212:docs/plan.md:742:- Desktop build from M15.
446a212:docs/plan.md:743:- Web build from M15.

     7

     9	
    10	## A. Kernel-boundary gates
    11	
    12	| Plan bullet | Evidence artifact | Produced by |
    13	|---|---|---|
    14	| `nmp-core` gains zero podcast nouns | `docs/perf/m11/kernel-boundary.md` with the grep output | `grep -RE 'Podcast\|Episode\|Transcript\|Chapter\|Player\|Feed\|Insight\|Guest\|RSS\|Audio\|MP3' crates/nmp-core/src/` (whitelist the `audio_playback.rs` trait file) → expected empty |

    16	| Reactivity behavior identical to Twitter slice | `docs/perf/m11/reactivity.md` | Re-run `reactivity-bench --standard --fail-on-gate` with `podcast-core` views registered alongside the existing nip01 views; assert all gates pass |
    17	| No app-state leaks across the boundary in either direction | same as row 1 (kernel) + a sibling grep across `crates/nmp-core/src/` for `nostr\|relay\|nip` produces no hit inside `apps/podcast/` crates | the grep is added to CI |
    18	
    19	---
    20	
    21	## B. Product-fidelity gates
    22	
    23	| Plan bullet | Evidence artifact | Produced by |
    24	|---|---|---|
    25	| UI parity: pixel diff ≤ 1 px per screen, font/rendering whitelisted | `docs/perf/m11/parity-screenshots.md` plus `docs/perf/m11/parity-screenshots/diff/*.png` (must be empty for all 20 screens) | `just screenshot-diff --fail-on-gate` (see [`screenshots.md`](screenshots.md)) |
    26	| Feature parity: every flow reproduced as scripted agent run | `docs/perf/m11/feature-flows.md` listing each `Lane<N>_<surface>.swift` XCUITest pass | XCUITest runs in CI on each PR + nightly aggregate report |
    27	| Subscribe to 10 real podcasts | `docs/perf/m11/subscriptions.md` with the 10 feed URLs + parsed metadata snapshot | Scripted: `just demo-subscribe-10` runs `SubscribePodcast` actions and dumps `PodcastRecord` JSON |

    29	| Play with background audio (lock screen) | `docs/perf/m11/background-audio.md` with screenshots of lock-screen controls + scripted seek/skip | manual test + simulator video recorded via `xcrun simctl io booted recordVideo` |
    30	| Resume playback after kill-relaunch | covered in the lane-4 XCUITest scenario `lane4-resume-after-kill.swift` | same scenario asserts post-relaunch `NowPlaying.payload.current_s == prev_position_s ± 1` |
    31	| Push notification on new-episode arrival | `docs/perf/m11/episode-push.md` with `xcrun simctl push` script + screenshot of the notification | scripted: refresh a feed where a new episode is injected; assert notification scheduled via `LocalNotificationCapability` |
    32	| Ask a question via **rig.rs** against a real non-Apple-Intelligence LLM endpoint (M11 multiplatform proof); separately also via Apple Intelligence on-device | `docs/perf/m11/ask-streaming.md` with first-token-latency on both routes; rig.rs path is **required**, not optional — it is the proof that the architecture works off iOS | benchmarked: `just demo-ask-stream --route rig` AND `just demo-ask-stream --route apple-intelligence` both pass |
    33	| Insights view generates structured summary on demand | `docs/perf/m11/insights-on-demand.md` showing the `RunInsight` action output structure | scripted scenario `lane5-run-insight.swift` |
    34	| Guest enrichment populates guest cards | `docs/perf/m11/guest-enrichment.md` showing before/after `GuestRecord.bio` | scripted scenario `lane6-guest-enrich.swift` |

    39	
    40	| Plan bullet | Evidence artifact | Produced by |
    41	|---|---|---|

    43	| Player UI updates every 250 ms without jank | `docs/perf/m11/player-tick.md` | scripted: `usePlayerSheet()` payload mutation rate measured against frame-time histogram |

    54	ios/NmpPodcast/NmpPodcastUITests/
    55	├── Lane1_Settings.swift

    67	
    68	crates/nmp-testing/tests/

    83	```
    84	
    85	Every test file ≤ 500 LOC. The cross-cutting kill-relaunch test is the most load-bearing — it asserts D2 (reactivity) and D4 (single writer per fact) hold under app termination at every state-transition boundary.
    86	
    87	---
    88	
    89	## E. CI binding
    90	
    91	`.github/workflows/m11-gates.yml` adds three new jobs:
    92	
    93	1. **`screenshot-diff`** — runs `just screenshot-diff --fail-on-gate` on a macOS-15 simulator host. Uploads diff PNGs as artifacts on failure.
    94	2. **`kernel-boundary`** — runs the grep above; fails if any matches.
    95	3. **`capability-review`** — runs `cargo run -p nmp-codegen -- validate-capabilities --capabilities-dir crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/capabilities/` — a new codegen subcommand that asserts request/result type definitions don't mention any podcast-domain noun (uses a curated wordlist + AST traversal).
    96	
    97	All three are required for merge to `master`.
    98	
    99	---
   100	
   101	## F. Sign-off
   102	
   103	M11 is **done** when:
   104	
   105	- Every row in §A / §B / §C above has a green evidence artifact.
   106	- The doctrine review at `docs/perf/m11/doctrine-review.md` signs off D0–D5 against the M11 surface (template: `docs/perf/m10.5/doctrine-review.md`).
   107	- `cargo test --workspace` is green.
   108	- `just screenshot-diff --fail-on-gate` is green.
   109	- The release artifact `ios/NmpPodcast.app` runs on iPhone 12 hardware (build + install + the 20 scripted scenarios run).
   110	- A tagged git commit `m11-podcast-app` on `master` represents the runnable artifact.
   111	
   112	The M11 report at `docs/perf/m11/podcast-app.md` is the index pointing at all of the above, written last.

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "git show 446a212:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md | nl -ba | sed -n '260,310p'" in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/.claude/worktrees/agent-a8c0acab50f455ada
 succeeded in 0ms:
   260	- `podcast.llm.anthropic_api_key`
   261	- `podcast.llm.local_base_url`
   262	
   263	**M11 provider configuration.** Because rig.rs is required in M11 (see §K), the API key must be reachable in M11. In M11 the key is injected via environment variables at test time:
   264	- `NMP_LLM_PROVIDER` — `openai` | `anthropic` | `local`
   265	- `NMP_LLM_MODEL` — e.g. `gpt-4o-mini`; populates `LlmRoute::RigProvider.model` for all providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, and Local); default `gpt-4o-mini` when unset with OpenAI
   266	- `NMP_LLM_API_KEY` — provider API key (OpenAI/Anthropic)
   267	- `NMP_LLM_BASE_URL` — required only for `local`; populates `RigProvider::Local { base_url }`
   268	
   269	The `KeyValueStoreCapability` bridge reads these vars at boot and writes them into the `podcast.llm.*` keys; the bootstrap also overwrites `SettingsRecord.llm_preferred_route` to `Rig { provider, model }`, so `select_route()` picks the rig path without any UI interaction. The Settings UI for end-user key entry is post-M11. On the iOS device in production, Apple Intelligence is the default and requires no key.
   270	
   271	Reads happen at action boot only. Per RMP bible commandment #2, keys never cross FFI as part of dispatch — they live on the platform side; the bridge resolves and injects them when executing the capability.
   272	
   273	---
   274	
   275	## J. Tests
   276	
   277	- Prompt-parity test: each `build_*` function called with the exact arguments from a reference transcript fixture → output asserted byte-equal to a checked-in `prompts_golden/*.txt`.
   278	- Router test table: each route selected per `(settings, capabilities)` combo.
   279	- Streaming integration test: `MockLlmCapability` emits tokens at a chosen cadence; assert `AskViewModule` deltas coalesce to ≤ 30 Hz; assert final `ChatTurn` content is the concatenation; assert citations parsed correctly.
   280	- Failure-mode test: capability emits `Error { reason: "rate_limit" }` mid-stream → `ChatTurn` commits with `is_error: true` and the `toast` field is set per doctrine D3.
   281	
   282	## K. rig.rs path — M11 required (not deferred)
   283	
   284	The rig.rs route through `rig-core` is **required to land and pass its end-to-end test in M11**. It is not deferred to M15 or any later milestone. Rationale: M11 is the kernel-boundary check; the rig.rs path is the proof that `podcast-llm` is genuinely multiplatform — not an iOS wrapper around Apple Intelligence with a Rust veneer.
   285	
   286	**M11 acceptance for rig.rs:**
   287	
   288	1. `just demo-ask-stream --route rig` runs against a real (non-Apple-Intelligence) LLM endpoint (OpenAI, Anthropic, or a local `ollama` instance) and streams a full answer for a test question over a fixture corpus.
   289	2. First-token latency ≤ 1500 ms on Wi-Fi (same gate as the Apple Intelligence path; documented in `docs/perf/m11/ask-streaming.md`).
   290	3. `crates/nmp-testing/tests/action_ask_streaming.rs` runs with `rig-core` as the provider (not `MockLlmCapability`) and passes.
   291	4. The integration test is in CI (`m11-gates.yml`) — not behind a `#[cfg(feature = "rig-e2e")]` that is never set in CI.
   292	
   293	Apple Intelligence remains the default iOS route. The rig.rs path is the non-Apple route that M11 must demonstrate. Both routes share the same action shape — the only difference is `select_route()`'s return value.
